34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.

40	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))

51	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
52	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)

296	  have hsplit : SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
297	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
298	      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
299	    refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
300	    have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
301	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
302	            chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
303	        = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
304	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
305	        (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
306	    exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm
307	  exact (IsSplitEpi.mk' ⟨_, hsplit⟩).epi

311	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
312	
313	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
314	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
315	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction.
316	
317	Construction (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): the glued sheaf is built directly as a
318	categorical limit — an **equalizer of pushforwards** — rather than a hand-built presheaf of
319	compatible families. Concretely, `glue` forms the two parallel maps
320	`∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{i,j} (ιᵢ ≫ f_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` (one leg the adjunction unit composed
321	with the pushforward-composition comparison, the other transported across the inverse
322	transition `(g_ij)⁻¹`), and takes their equalizer inside `Scheme.Modules D.glued`. The
323	self-identity (C1) and triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) hypotheses `_hC1`/`_hC2` on the
324	family `g` are NOT consumed in forming the equalizer object (the limit exists for any family
325	of transition maps); they are the descent conditions pinned down downstream when the
326	restriction isomorphisms are produced. The body below and the `_hC1`/`_hC2` signature are
327	complete (axiom-clean since iter-056). -/
328	
329	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
330	
331	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
332	
333	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
334	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
335	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
336	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
337	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
338	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
339	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
340	
341	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
342	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
343	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
344	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
345	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
346	
347	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
348	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
349	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
350	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
351	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
352	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
353	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
354	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
355	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
356	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
357	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
358	
359	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
360	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
361	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
362	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
363	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
364	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
365	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
366	
367	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
368	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
369	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
370	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
371	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
372	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
373	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
374	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
375	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
376	    Category.assoc]
377	
378	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
379	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
380	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
381	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
382	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
383	the module cocycle (C2). -/
384	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
385	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
386	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
387	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
388	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
389	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
390	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
391	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
392	
393	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
394	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
395	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
396	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
397	
398	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
399	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
400	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
401	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
402	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
403	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
404	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
405	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
406	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
407	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
408	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
409	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
410	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
411	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
412	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
413	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
414	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
415	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
416	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
417	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
418	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
419	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
420	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
421	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
422	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
423	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
424	    D.glued.Modules :=
425	  -- **Effective descent as an equalizer of pushforwards.** The glued sheaf is the
426	  -- equalizer of the two canonical maps `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{ij} (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)`
427	  -- (`j_ij = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ : V_ij ↪ X`): the first map restricts the `i`-th chart section to
428	  -- `V_ij`, the second restricts the `j`-th and transports it across the transition `g_ij`,
429	  -- using the glue condition `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ιⱼ = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ`. The cocycle hypotheses
430	  -- `_hC1`/`_hC2` are not needed to *construct* the object (they pin down the chart
431	  -- restriction isomorphisms `glueRestrictionIso`, built downstream). Pushforward preserves
432	  -- the sheaf condition and limits, so this equalizer of sheaves of modules is again a sheaf.
433	  let Qfun : D.J × D.J → D.glued.Modules := fun p =>
434	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
435	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
436	  let P : D.glued.Modules := ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
437	  -- first leg: restrict the `p.1`-chart section to the overlap `V (p.1, p.2)`
438	  let aComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
439	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).obj (M p.1) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
440	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
441	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
442	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
443	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
444	  -- second leg: restrict the `p.2`-chart section, transport it across `g`, and reindex
445	  -- the immersion via the glue condition
446	  let bComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
447	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).obj (M p.2) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
448	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
449	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
450	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
451	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
452	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
453	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
454	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
455	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
456	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
457	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
458	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
459	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
460	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
461	  equalizer a b

463	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
464	
465	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
466	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
467	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
468	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
469	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
470	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
471	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
472	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
473	morphisms. -/
474	
475	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
476	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
477	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
478	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
479	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
480	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
481	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
482	  constructor
483	  intro V
484	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
485	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
486	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
487	  apply zigzag_isConnected
488	  intro s t
489	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
490	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
491	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
492	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
493	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
494	
495	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
496	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
497	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
498	Project-local. -/
499	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
500	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
501	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
502	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
503	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
504	
505	/-- The free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism: equal morphisms give
506	`pullbackFreeIso`s related by the `eqToHom` transport of their (differing) sources.
507	Project-local — used for the bundle-transition self-identity. -/
508	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
509	    eqToHom (congrArg
510	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
511	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
512	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
513	  subst h; simp
514	
515	/-- Iso-level free-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`, the composite
516	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the
517	(differing) pullback sources. Proved generically (`φ`, `ψ` variables, `subst`), so applying
518	it never forces the kernel to whnf a concrete immersion — the leaner replacement for the
519	`.hom`-level cast chain in `bundleTransition_self`. Project-local. -/
520	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
521	    (I : Type u) :
522	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
523	      = eqToIso (congrArg
524	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))
525	          h) := by
526	  subst h; simp
527	
528	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
529	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
530	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
531	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
532	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
533	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
534	Project-local. -/
535	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
536	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
537	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
538	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
539	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
540	  intro i
541	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
542	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
543	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
544	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
545	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
546	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
547	
548	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
549	
550	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
551	
552	/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle
553	
554	The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
555	along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
556	`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
557	constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
558	multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
559	overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
560	
561	/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
562	(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
563	lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
564	    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
565	  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
566	    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
567	  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]
568	
569	/-- The injected Cramer inverse and minor matrices over the overlap structure sheaf are
570	mutually inverse — the `GL_d` invertibility hypotheses for `matrixToFreeIso`. Project-local. -/
571	private lemma bundleMatrix_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
572	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
573	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
574	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
575	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) *
576	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
577	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
578	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 ∧
579	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
580	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
581	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) *
582	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
583	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
584	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 := by
585	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
586	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one]
587	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]
588	
589	/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
590	sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
591	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
592	`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
593	matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
594	free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
595	noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
596	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
597	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj

1135	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
1136	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`

1138	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
1139	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
1140	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.

11	/-!
12	# Section graded ring infrastructure, Layer 1: tensor powers of a sheaf of modules
13	
14	This file builds the Mathlib-absent infrastructure of
15	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`, Layer 1
16	(`sec:sgr_tensor_powers`): the tensor product, tensor powers, and twists of
17	sheaves of modules over a scheme `X`, together with the unitor and braiding
18	isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product.
19	
20	The category `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` of sheaves of modules
21	over a scheme carries **no** monoidal structure in Mathlib (the structure sheaf
22	varies the base ring over opens).  Mathlib *does* supply:
23	
24	* the symmetric monoidal structure on the category of **presheaves** of modules
25	  `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
26	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal`), and
27	* the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`
28	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification`).
29	
30	We therefore build the tensor product of sheaves of modules as the sheafification
31	of the objectwise (presheaf) tensor product, following
32	[Stacks, Tag 01CA].
33	
34	## Main definitions
35	
36	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification` — the scheme-level
37	  sheafification functor `X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules`.
38	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) —
39	  `F ⊗ G := (F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)^#`.
40	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow` (`def:sheafTensorPow`) —
41	  the `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`) —
43	  the `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}`.
44	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafificationCounitIso` — the reflective
45	  counit iso `(F.toPresheaf)^# ≅ F`.
46	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjUnitIso`,
47	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjRightUnitor`,
48	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorBraiding` — the left/right unitor and
49	  braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product.
50	
51	The comparison isomorphism `L^{⊗m} ⊗ L^{⊗m'} ≅ L^{⊗(m+m')}`
52	(`lem:sheafTensorPow_add`) is **deferred**: see the handoff note before the end
53	of the file for the single missing ingredient (strong-monoidality of the module
54	sheafification functor) and the launching pad assembled here.
55	-/

65	/-- The scheme-level sheafification functor, sending a presheaf of modules over a
66	scheme `X` to its associated sheaf of modules `X.Modules`.  It is the
67	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification` functor for the identity morphism of the
68	underlying presheaf of rings (which is locally bijective).  Non-private because it
69	appears in the statement of `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
70	noncomputable def sheafification : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules :=
71	  PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
72	
73	/-- The category `X.PresheafOfModules` of presheaves of modules over a scheme,
74	presented in the exact form `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)`
75	for which Mathlib equips it with a symmetric monoidal structure.  This is
76	*definitionally* `X.PresheafOfModules` (since
77	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`), so a term of
78	either type is accepted for the other. -/
79	private abbrev MonoidalPresheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ :=
80	  _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
81	
82	/-- The tensor product of two sheaves of modules over a scheme, defined as the
83	sheafification of the objectwise tensor product presheaf
84	(Mathlib `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  See [Stacks, Tag 01CA]
85	(`def:sheafTensorObj`). -/
86	noncomputable def tensorObj (F G : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
87	  sheafification.obj
88	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
89	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
90	
91	/-- The structure sheaf as a sheaf of modules over itself: the unit object of the
92	tensor product, i.e. the zeroth tensor power `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X`
93	(`lem:moduleUnit_mathlib`). -/
94	private noncomputable abbrev unitModule (X : Scheme.{u}) : X.Modules :=
95	  SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf
96	
97	/-- The `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules over a scheme, defined
98	by recursion: `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X` (the unit module) and
99	`L^{⊗(m+1)} = L^{⊗m} ⊗ L`.  See [Stacks, Tag 01CU] (`def:sheafTensorPow`). -/
100	noncomputable def tensorPow (L : X.Modules) : ℕ → X.Modules
101	  | 0 => unitModule X
102	  | (m + 1) => tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L
103	
104	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_zero (L : X.Modules) : tensorPow L 0 = unitModule X := rfl
105	
106	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_succ (L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) :
107	    tensorPow L (m + 1) = tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L := rfl
108	
109	/-- The `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules `F` by the `m`-th
110	tensor power of a line bundle `L` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`).  This is the
111	degree-`m` carrier of the section graded module. -/
112	noncomputable def moduleTensorPow (F L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) : X.Modules :=
113	  tensorObj F (tensorPow L m)
114	
115	@[simp] private lemma moduleTensorPow_zero (F L : X.Modules) :
116	    moduleTensorPow F L 0 = tensorObj F (unitModule X) := rfl

118	/-! ### Unitor and braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product
119	
120	These are the parts of the (would-be) symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
121	that descend through sheafification from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
122	using only *functoriality* of `sheafification` (and, for the unitors, the
123	reflective counit iso) — no strong-monoidality of `sheafification` is needed, so
124	they are axiom-clean.  They are the launching pad for `tensorPowAdd`. -/

126	/-- The counit isomorphism of the module sheafification adjunction: sheafifying
127	the underlying presheaf of a sheaf of modules returns the sheaf itself.  This is
128	an isomorphism because the counit of `sheafification ⊣ toPresheafOfModules` is
129	invertible (the right adjoint `SheafOfModules.forget` is fully faithful).  It is
130	the launching pad for the left-unitor base case of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
131	private noncomputable def sheafificationCounitIso (G : X.Modules) :
132	    sheafification.obj ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ≅ G :=
133	  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
134	    (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).counit).app G

136	/-- The left-unitor isomorphism `unitModule X ⊗ G ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
137	product: the presheaf left unitor `λ_` descended through sheafification, composed
138	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  This is the base case (`m = 0`) of
139	`tensorPowAdd`.  Axiom-clean. -/
140	private noncomputable def tensorObjUnitIso (G : X.Modules) :
141	    tensorObj (unitModule X) G ≅ G :=
142	  sheafification.mapIso
143	      (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
144	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
145	    sheafificationCounitIso G
146	
147	/-- The right-unitor isomorphism `G ⊗ unitModule X ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
148	product: the presheaf right unitor `ρ_` descended through sheafification, composed
149	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  Axiom-clean (no monoidal structure
150	on `X.Modules` is required). -/
151	private noncomputable def tensorObjRightUnitor (G : X.Modules) :
152	    tensorObj G (unitModule X) ≅ G :=
153	  sheafification.mapIso
154	      (MonoidalCategory.rightUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
155	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
156	    sheafificationCounitIso G
157	
158	/-- The braiding isomorphism `F ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ F` of the sheaf tensor product,
159	descended through sheafification from the symmetric braiding on
160	`X.PresheafOfModules` (`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  Axiom-clean: the
161	braiding is pure sheafification-functoriality of the presheaf-level braiding, so
162	no monoidal structure on `X.Modules` is required.  This is the symmetry used in
163	the inductive step of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
164	private noncomputable def tensorBraiding (F G : X.Modules) :
165	    tensorObj F G ≅ tensorObj G F :=
166	  sheafification.mapIso
167	    (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
168	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
169	
170	/-! ### Lax-monoidal global sections: the section multiplication
171	
172	The global-sections functor `Γ(X, -)` is only *lax* monoidal: a pair of global
173	sections does not commute with sheafification, so the multiplication is a map,
174	not an isomorphism.  It is nonetheless `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)`-linear and is the data the
175	section graded ring is built from. -/
176	
177	/-- The **section multiplication** (`def:sectionMul`), the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear map
178	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G) → Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.
179	
180	Its domain `(F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)(X)` is, by the objectwise formula of
181	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-module
182	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G)` of elementary tensors of global sections; a pair
183	`(σ, τ)` is sent to `σ ⊗ τ`.  Postcomposing with the global-sections component of
184	the sheafification unit `η : P → P^#` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) lands in
185	`Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.  As a morphism in `ModuleCat (Γ(X,𝒪_X))` it is automatically
186	`Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear; this records that linearity.  Axiom-clean: it is pure
187	sheafification-unit naturality, requiring no monoidal structure on `X.Modules`. -/
188	noncomputable def sectionsMul (F G : X.Modules) :
189	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
190	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).obj (Opposite.op ⊤) ⟶
191	      (tensorObj F G).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
192	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
193	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
194	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))).app (Opposite.op ⊤)
195	
196	/-! ### The strong-monoidality comparison `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`
197	
198	Following `analogies/snap-route.md` (Analogue 1) and the blueprint proof of
199	`lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: module sheafification is the
200	localization functor at the class `W := J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)` of
201	morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying abelian-presheaf morphism is a
202	local isomorphism (a `J.W` for the opens topology `J` on `X`). -/
203	
204	/-- The Grothendieck topology on the opens of the scheme `X`. -/
205	private abbrev opensTopology (X : Scheme.{u}) : GrothendieckTopology (TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :=
206	  Opens.grothendieckTopology (X : TopCat)
207	
208	open MorphismProperty in
209	/-- **Localization criterion for module sheafification.**  The scheme-level module
210	sheafification `sheafification.map f` is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules iff the
211	underlying abelian-presheaf morphism `(PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map f` lies in
212	the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology (i.e. is a local isomorphism of
213	abelian-group presheaves).  This is the reduction step of
214	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: it turns the strong-monoidality comparison
215	into a purely abelian local-isomorphism statement.  Project-local: it specialises
216	`_root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` to the
217	identity morphism of `X.ringCatSheaf.obj`. -/
218	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_iff {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules} (f : P ⟶ Q) :
219	    IsIso (sheafification.map f) ↔
220	      (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map f) := by
221	  have e := _root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms
222	      (J := opensTopology X) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
223	  constructor
224	  · intro h
225	    have h' : ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)).inverseImage
226	        (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj))) f := h
227	    rw [← e] at h'
228	    exact h'
229	  · intro h
230	    have h' : (((opensTopology X).W).inverseImage
231	        (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) f := h
232	    rw [e] at h'
233	    exact h'
234	
235	/-- **The sheafification unit is an abelian local isomorphism.**  The underlying
236	abelian-presheaf morphism of the module sheafification unit `η_P : P ⟶ P^#` is
237	*definitionally* the abelian sheafification unit `toSheafify J P.presheaf`
238	(`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app`), which lies
239	in the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology by
240	`GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify`.  Project-local: this is the `η_P ∈ J.W`
241	ingredient of the abelian-`J.W`-monoidality transfer underlying
242	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
243	lemma localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
244	    (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
245	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) := by
246	  rw [PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
247	  exact (opensTopology X).W_toSheafify _
248	
249	/-- **Sheafification inverts the localization unit.**  `sheafification.map η_P` is an
250	isomorphism of sheaves of modules (the reflective-localization unit becomes invertible
251	after sheafifying).  Obtained by feeding `localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit` through the
252	localization criterion `isIso_sheafification_map_iff`.  Project-local: the `m = 0`
253	launching pad and the un-whiskered special case of
254	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
255	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
256	    IsIso (sheafification.map
257	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) :=
258	  (isIso_sheafification_map_iff _).mpr (localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit P)
259	
260	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — relative tensor product as a coequalizer
261	
262	This section builds the **objectwise** content of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`
263	(`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`): over a commutative ring `S` and `S`-modules `M, N`,
264	the relative tensor product `M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer, *in the category of abelian
265	groups*, of the two `S`-action maps
266	
267	  `M ⊗[ℤ] S ⊗[ℤ] N  ⇉  M ⊗[ℤ] N`,    `m ⊗ s ⊗ n ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`  /  `m ⊗ (s • n)`.
268	
269	This is the Mathlib-absent brick on which the strong-monoidality comparison
270	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit` rests: the underlying abelian presheaf of the
271	presheaf-level relative tensor `P ⊗_p Q` is, objectwise, exactly this coequalizer.  The
272	universal property is the abelian-group universal property of the relative tensor product,
273	packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`.  Everything here is axiom-clean.
274	
275	The promotion of this objectwise colimit to the presheaf category `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrp`
276	(where colimits are computed objectwise) and the identification of the whiskered unit
277	`η_P ▷ Q` with the induced map of coequalizers are the next steps; see the handoff note. -/
278	
279	namespace RelativeTensorCoequalizer
280	
281	open TensorProduct
282	
283	variable (S : Type u) [CommRing S] (M N : Type u)
284	  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
285	
286	/-- The `S`-action map `S ⊗[ℤ] N → N`, `s ⊗ n ↦ s • n`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
287	noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
288	  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
289	    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
290	    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
291	
292	/-- The `S`-action map `M ⊗[ℤ] S → M`, `m ⊗ s ↦ s • m`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
293	noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
294	  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
295	    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
296	    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
297	
298	/-- Right action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`. -/
299	noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
300	  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
301	
302	/-- Left action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`. -/
303	noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
304	  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp
305	    (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
306	
307	omit [Module S M] in
308	@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
309	    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
310	
311	omit [Module S N] in
312	@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
313	    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
314	
315	/-- The canonical projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N`, `m ⊗ n ↦ m ⊗ n`, as a `ℤ`-linear
316	map.  It is the cofork map exhibiting `M ⊗[S] N` as the coequalizer. -/
317	noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
318	  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
319	    { toFun := fun m =>
320	        (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
321	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
322	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp }
323	    (fun r m n => by simp)).toIntLinearMap
324	
325	@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
326	
327	/-- The projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` is surjective (it is the canonical
328	quotient map onto the relative tensor). -/
329	lemma projL_surjective : Function.Surjective (projL S M N) := by
330	  intro y
331	  induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
332	  | zero => exact ⟨0, map_zero _⟩
333	  | tmul m n => exact ⟨m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n, projL_tmul S M N m n⟩
334	  | add a b ha hb =>
335	    obtain ⟨pa, rfl⟩ := ha; obtain ⟨pb, rfl⟩ := hb; exact ⟨pa + pb, map_add _ _ _⟩
336	
337	/-- The two action maps become equal after the projection: this is the cofork
338	coequalizing condition, established at the level of `ℤ`-linear maps. -/
339	lemma projL_comp_act :
340	    (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
341	  apply TensorProduct.ext'; intro m x
342	  induction x with
343	  | zero => rw [tmul_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
344	  | tmul s n =>
345	    change projL S M N (actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
346	      = projL S M N (actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
347	    rw [actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul, projL_tmul, ← TensorProduct.smul_tmul',
348	      TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
349	  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]
350	
351	/-- Left action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
352	noncomputable def aL :
353	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
354	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
355	/-- Right action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
356	noncomputable def aR :
357	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
358	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
359	/-- The projection as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
360	noncomputable def piMor :
361	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N) :=
362	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
363	
364	@[simp] lemma piMor_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (piMor S M N)) x = projL S M N x := rfl
365	
366	instance piMor_epi : Epi (piMor S M N) :=
367	  ConcreteCategory.epi_of_surjective (piMor S M N) (projL_surjective S M N)
368	
369	/-- The projection coequalizes the two action maps (as morphisms of abelian groups). -/
370	lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
371	  ext x; exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x
372	
373	/-- The cofork `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` of abelian groups. -/
374	noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
375	  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)
376	
377	/-- The descent map out of `M ⊗[S] N` induced by a cofork `s`: a pair of global
378	sections balanced under the `S`-action factors through the relative tensor.  This
379	is the universal property packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`. -/
380	noncomputable def descHom (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
381	    (M ⊗[S] N) →+ s.pt :=
382	  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
383	    { toFun := fun m =>
384	        { toFun := fun n => (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
385	          map_zero' := by rw [tmul_zero, map_zero]
386	          map_add' := fun n1 n2 => by rw [tmul_add, map_add] }
387	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp [zero_tmul]
388	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [add_tmul] }
389	    (fun a m n => by
390	      simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
391	      have key :=
392	        congrArg (fun φ => (ConcreteCategory.hom φ) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n))) s.condition
393	      simpa using key)
394	
395	@[simp] lemma descHom_tmul (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) (m : M) (n : N) :
396	    descHom S M N s (m ⊗ₜ[S] n) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n) := rfl
397	
398	/-- The descent map as a morphism of abelian groups out of the cofork apex. -/
399	noncomputable def descMor (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
400	    (cofork S M N).pt ⟶ s.pt :=
401	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (descHom S M N s)
402	
403	/-- The descent map factors the cofork's projection: `π ≫ descMor s = s.π`. -/
404	lemma descFac (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
405	    (cofork S M N).π ≫ descMor S M N s = s.π := by
406	  ext x
407	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
408	  | zero => simp
409	  | tmul m n =>
410	    change descHom S M N s (projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
411	    rw [projL_tmul, descHom_tmul]
412	  | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
413	
414	/-- **`M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer**, in the category of abelian groups, of the two
415	`S`-action maps `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N`.  This is the objectwise content of
416	`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`; uniqueness uses that the projection `piMor` is an
417	epimorphism.  Axiom-clean. -/
418	noncomputable def isColimitCofork : Limits.IsColimit (cofork S M N) :=
419	  Limits.Cofork.IsColimit.mk _ (descMor S M N) (descFac S M N)
420	    (fun s _ hf => (cancel_epi (piMor S M N)).mp (hf.trans (descFac S M N s).symm))
421	
422	end RelativeTensorCoequalizer
423	
424	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — presheaf promotion of the coequalizer (Step 1)
425	
426	The objectwise coequalizer `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork` exhibits, for a fixed
427	open `U`, the relative tensor `Γ(U,P) ⊗_{R(U)} Γ(U,Q)` as a coequalizer of the two
428	`R(U)`-action maps on `Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ R(U) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q) ⇉ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q)`.  To promote this
429	to the functor category `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` (where colimits are computed objectwise, via
430	`CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits`) one first needs the two **domain
431	presheaves of the cofork as honest functors**, whose restriction maps are the `ℤ`-tensors of
432	the underlying restriction maps.  This section builds the first of those two functors
433	(`relTensorDomainPresheaf`, the `Γ(-,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(-,Q)` presheaf); it is the concrete Step-1 brick
434	of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer` (`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`).
435	
436	See the handoff note at the end of the file for the verified recipe for the remaining pieces
437	(triple-tensor presheaf, the natural action/projection transformations, the colimit lift, and
438	the apex identification) and the heartbeat/coercion friction points that must be budgeted. -/
439	
440	open scoped TensorProduct
441	
442	/-- Restriction map for a presheaf of modules with syntactic `↥(P.obj U)` carriers.
443	The underlying function is `(P.presheaf.map f).hom`; the type annotation forces the
444	domain/codomain to print as `↥(P.obj U)` / `↥(P.obj V)` (not `↥((P.presheaf).obj U)`,
445	which are rfl-defeq but syntactically distinct).  The syntactic agreement is the
446	load-bearing ingredient for `TensorProduct.map_tmul` unification in
447	`relTensorActL.naturality` / `relTensorActR.naturality`. -/
448	private noncomputable def objRestrict (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
449	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
450	    ↥(P.obj U) →ₗ[ℤ] ↥(P.obj V) :=
451	  (show ↥(P.obj U) →+ ↥(P.obj V) from
452	    { toFun := (P.presheaf.map f).hom
453	      map_zero' := map_zero (P.presheaf.map f).hom
454	      map_add' := map_add (P.presheaf.map f).hom }).toIntLinearMap
455	
456	@[simp] private lemma objRestrict_apply (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
457	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (x : ↥(P.obj U)) :
458	    objRestrict P f x = (P.presheaf.map f).hom x := rfl
459	
460	/-- Identity law for the syntactic-carrier restriction: `objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = id`. -/
461	private lemma objRestrict_id (P : X.PresheafOfModules) (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) :
462	    objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = LinearMap.id := by
463	  ext x
464	  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id,
465	    AddMonoidHom.id_apply, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
466	
467	/-- Composition law for the syntactic-carrier restriction:
468	`objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g) ∘ (objRestrict P f)`. -/
469	private lemma objRestrict_comp (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
470	    {U V W : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (g : V ⟶ W) :
471	    objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g).comp (objRestrict P f) := by
472	  ext x
473	  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp,
474	    AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]
475	
476	/-- The objectwise `ℤ`-tensor presheaf `U ↦ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q)` of two presheaves of modules
477	over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the `ℤ`-tensors of the
478	two underlying restriction maps.  This is the codomain (apex-adjacent) presheaf of the cofork
479	in the presheaf promotion of `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork`; it is the concrete
480	Step-1 brick of the presheaf-level coequalizer iso `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`
481	(`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).  Project-local: no objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of
482	abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib (`AddCommGrpCat` carries no monoidal
483	structure in the current pin). -/
484	noncomputable def relTensorDomainPresheaf (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
485	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab where
486	  obj U := AddCommGrpCat.of (P.obj U ⊗[ℤ] Q.obj U)
487	  map {U V} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
488	    (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).toAddMonoidHom
489	  map_id U := by
490	    ext x
491	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
492	    | zero => simp
493	    | tmul m n =>
494	      simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
495	        objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id,
496	        AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
497	    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
498	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
499	    ext x
500	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
501	    | zero => simp
502	    | tmul m n =>
503	      simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
504	        objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp,
505	        AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
506	    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]

508	/-- The objectwise `ℤ`-tensor triple presheaf `U ↦ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ (𝒪_X(U) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q))` of two
509	presheaves of modules over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the
510	`ℤ`-tensors of the underlying restriction maps (the middle factor restricting via the ring
511	restriction map of `𝒪_X`).  This is the **domain** row of the relative-tensor coequalizer
512	presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`); objectwise it is the triple tensor on which
513	the two `R(U)`-action maps `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`/`actRmap` act.  Project-local: no
514	objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib. -/
515	noncomputable def relTensorTriplePresheaf (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
516	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab where
517	  obj U := AddCommGrpCat.of (P.obj U ⊗[ℤ] (X.sheaf.obj.obj U ⊗[ℤ] Q.obj U))
518	  map {U V} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
519	    (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
520	      (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
521	        (objRestrict Q f))).toAddMonoidHom
522	  map_id U := by
523	    have hR : (X.sheaf.obj.map (𝟙 U)).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap =
524	        LinearMap.id (R := ℤ) (M := ↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) := by
525	      ext s
526	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, CommRingCat.hom_id, RingHom.toAddMonoidHom_eq_coe,
527	        AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
528	      rfl
529	    rw [objRestrict_id P U, objRestrict_id Q U, hR, TensorProduct.map_id, TensorProduct.map_id]
530	    rfl
531	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
532	    have hR : (X.sheaf.obj.map (f ≫ g)).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap =
533	        ((X.sheaf.obj.map g).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap).comp
534	          ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap) := by
535	      ext s
536	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, CommRingCat.hom_comp,
537	        RingHom.toAddMonoidHom_eq_coe, AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap, LinearMap.coe_comp,
538	        Function.comp_apply]
539	      rfl
540	    rw [objRestrict_comp P f g, objRestrict_comp Q f g, hR, TensorProduct.map_comp,
541	      TensorProduct.map_comp]
542	    rfl

544	/-- The **left-action** natural transformation of the coequalizer rows
545	(`def:relTensorActL`): `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`, whose
546	component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map
547	`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap` collapsing the middle ring factor through the scalar
548	action of `𝒪_X(U)` on `Γ(U,P)`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality in `U` is the
549	compatibility of the module action with the restriction maps, checked on elementary tensors
550	by `⊗`-induction (the single fact `PresheafOfModules.map_smul`, bridged to the abelian
551	restriction by `objRestrict_apply`). -/
552	noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
553	    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
554	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
555	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
556	  naturality {U V} f := by
557	    -- The underlying ℤ-linear naturality square, proven by `⊗`-induction.  The single
558	    -- mathematical fact is `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` (semilinearity of the restriction).
559	    have key :
560	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↥(P.obj V)) (↥(Q.obj V))).comp
561	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
562	              (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
563	                (objRestrict Q f)))
564	          = (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).comp
565	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↥(P.obj U))
566	                (↥(Q.obj U))) := by
567	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
568	      intro m y
569	      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
570	      | zero => simp
571	      | tmul s n =>
572	        change ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap s • objRestrict P f m)
573	              ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f n
574	            = objRestrict P f (s • m) ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f n
575	        congr 1
576	        rw [objRestrict_apply, objRestrict_apply]
577	        exact (PresheafOfModules.map_smul P f s m).symm
578	      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb, TensorProduct.tmul_add]
579	    -- Transport the linear-map square to the categorical naturality square in `Ab`.
580	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
581	    dsimp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf]
582	    ext z
583	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
584	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
585	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply] using hz
586	
587	/-- The **right-action** natural transformation of the coequalizer rows
588	(`def:relTensorActR`): `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`, whose
589	component at `U` is the objectwise right-action map
590	`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap` collapsing the middle ring factor through the scalar
591	action of `𝒪_X(U)` on `Γ(U,Q)`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`.  Naturality is the
592	compatibility of the module action with the restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`
593	on `Q`), checked on elementary tensors by `⊗`-induction. -/
594	noncomputable def relTensorActR (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
595	    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
596	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
597	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
598	  naturality {U V} f := by
599	    have key :
600	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↥(P.obj V)) (↥(Q.obj V))).comp
601	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
602	              (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
603	                (objRestrict Q f)))
604	          = (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).comp
605	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↥(P.obj U))
606	                (↥(Q.obj U))) := by
607	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
608	      intro m y
609	      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
610	      | zero => simp
611	      | tmul s n =>
612	        change objRestrict P f m
613	              ⊗ₜ[ℤ] ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap s • objRestrict Q f n)
614	            = objRestrict P f m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f (s • n)
615	        congr 1
616	        rw [objRestrict_apply, objRestrict_apply]
617	        exact (PresheafOfModules.map_smul Q f s n).symm
618	      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb, TensorProduct.tmul_add]
619	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
620	    dsimp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf]
621	    ext z
622	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
623	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
624	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply] using hz

626	/-- The **projection** natural transformation (`relTensorProj`):
627	`relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q ⟶ (toPresheaf).obj (P ⊗_p Q)`, whose component at `U` is the
628	canonical quotient `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL` from the objectwise `ℤ`-tensor onto the
629	relative tensor `Γ(U,P) ⊗_{𝒪_X(U)} Γ(U,Q)` (the apex of the cofork, identified with the value of
630	the presheaf monoidal tensor by `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj`).  This is the cofork
631	map of the presheaf-level coequalizer presentation `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`. -/
632	noncomputable def relTensorProj (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
633	    relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q ⟶
634	      (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj
635	        (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X) P Q) where
636	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
637	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
638	  naturality {U V} f := by
639	    -- NATURALITY (the square `projL_V ∘ domain.map f = apex.map f ∘ projL_U`).  We prove the
640	    -- underlying `ℤ`-linear square as `key` and transport it to the categorical square in `Ab`.
641	    -- An element-level `⊗`-induction at the `Ab` level is blocked by the `AddCommGrpCat.of` carrier
642	    -- instance mismatch (`map_add` fails to fire on the bundled `Ab`-morphism applied to `a + b`);
643	    -- working with bare `ℤ`-linear maps and `TensorProduct.ext'` sidesteps it entirely.  On an
644	    -- elementary tensor `m ⊗ₜ n` both composites send it to
645	    -- `(objRestrict P f m) ⊗ₜ[R(V)] (objRestrict Q f n)` definitionally: the LHS via
646	    -- `TensorProduct.map`+`projL`, the RHS via `projL`+`tensorObj_map_tmul`
647	    -- (both `⊗ₜ`-on-the-nose).  The `S = X.sheaf.obj.obj V` vs `R.obj V` base-ring discrepancy is a
648	    -- `forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`-identity, so the elementary-tensor case is `rfl` (no instance
649	    -- re-synthesis, since the existing goal instances are reused).
650	    have key :
651	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (↑(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↑(P.obj V)) (↑(Q.obj V))).comp
652	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f))
653	          = (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom
654	                (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj
655	                  (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X) P Q)).map
656	                    f)).toIntLinearMap.comp
657	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (↑(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↑(P.obj U))
658	                (↑(Q.obj U))) := by
659	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
660	      intro m n
661	      rfl
662	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
663	    ext z
664	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
665	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
666	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply, AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap] using hz
